content.thinking: Let me start by gathering all the required information about the CockroachDB cluster configuration. I'll run multiple commands in parallel to collect the data efficiently, then compile it into a ConfigMap.

content.text: I'll gather all the cluster configuration data in parallel, then write the findings to a ConfigMap.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure 2>&1
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT variable, value FROM crdb_internal.cluster_settings WHERE value != default_value;" 2>&1
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT target, raw_config_sql FROM crdb_internal.zones;" 2>&1

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret cockroachdb-client-root -o jsonpath='{.data}' 2>&1 | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print([k for k in d.keys()])"
input.description: List keys in client root secret

content.content: ['ca.crt', 'client.root.crt', 'client.root.key']

content.text: The pod has client certs at `/cockroach/cockroach-certs/`. Let me connect using those certs.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs 2>&1
input.description: Get node status with TLS certs

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SELECT variable, value FROM crdb_internal.cluster_settings WHERE value != default_value;" 2>&1
input.description: Get non-default cluster settings with TLS

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SELECT variable, value, default_value, description FROM crdb_internal.cluster_settings WHERE variable LIKE 'sql.defaults%' ORDER BY variable;" 2>&1

content.content:
variable	value	default_value	description
sql.defaults.cost_scans_with_default_col_size.enabled	false	false	setting to true uses the same size for all columns to compute scan cost
sql.defaults.datestyle	iso, mdy	iso, mdy	default value for DateStyle session setting [iso, mdy = 0, iso, dmy = 1, iso, ymd = 2]
sql.defaults.default_hash_sharded_index_bucket_count	16	16	used as bucket count if bucket count is not specified in hash sharded index definition
sql.defaults.default_int_size	8	8	the size, in bytes, of an INT type
sql.defaults.disallow_full_table_scans.enabled	false	false	setting to true rejects queries that have planned a full table scan
sql.defaults.distsql	auto	auto	default distributed SQL execution mode [off = 0, auto = 1, on = 2, always = 3]
sql.defaults.experimental_alter_column_type.enabled	false	false	default value for experimental_alter_column_type session setting; enables the use of ALTER COLUMN TYPE for general conversions
sql.defaults.experimental_auto_rehoming.enabled	false	false	default value for experimental_enable_auto_rehoming; allows for rows in REGIONAL BY ROW tables to be auto-rehomed on UPDATE
sql.defaults.experimental_computed_column_rewrites			allows rewriting computed column expressions in CREATE TABLE and ALTER TABLE statements; the format is: '(before expression) -> (after expression) [, (before expression) -> (after expression) ...]'
sql.defaults.experimental_distsql_planning	off	off	default experimental_distsql_planning mode; enables experimental opt-driven DistSQL planning [off = 0, on = 1]
sql.defaults.experimental_enable_unique_without_index_constraints.enabled	false	false	default value for experimental_enable_unique_without_index_constraints session setting;disables unique without index constraints by default
sql.defaults.experimental_implicit_column_partitioning.enabled	false	false	default value for experimental_enable_temp_tables; allows for the use of implicit column partitioning
sql.defaults.experimental_temporary_tables.enabled	false	false	default value for experimental_enable_temp_tables; allows for use of temporary tables by default
sql.defaults.foreign_key_cascades_limit	10000	10000	default value for foreign_key_cascades_limit session setting; limits the number of cascading operations that run as part of a single query
sql.defaults.idle_in_session_timeout	0s	0s	default value for the idle_in_session_timeout; default value for the idle_in_session_timeout session setting; controls the duration a session is permitted to idle before the session is terminated; if set to 0, there is no timeout
sql.defaults.idle_in_transaction_session_timeout	0s	0s	default value for the idle_in_transaction_session_timeout; controls the duration a session is permitted to idle in a transaction before the session is terminated; if set to 0, there is no timeout
sql.defaults.implicit_select_for_update.enabled	true	true	default value for enable_implicit_select_for_update session setting; enables FOR UPDATE locking during the row-fetch phase of mutation statements
sql.defaults.insert_fast_path.enabled	true	true	default value for enable_insert_fast_path session setting; enables a specialized insert path
sql.defaults.intervalstyle	postgres	postgres	default value for IntervalStyle session setting [postgres = 0, iso_8601 = 1, sql_standard = 2]
sql.defaults.large_full_scan_rows	1000	1000	default value for large_full_scan_rows session setting which determines the maximum table size allowed for a full scan when disallow_full_table_scans is set to true
sql.defaults.locality_optimized_partitioned_index_scan.enabled	true	true	default value for locality_optimized_partitioned_index_scan session setting; enables searching for rows in the current region before searching remote regions
sql.defaults.lock_timeout	0s	0s	default value for the lock_timeout; default value for the lock_timeout session setting; controls the duration a query is permitted to wait while attempting to acquire a lock on a key or while blocking on an existing lock in order to perform a non-locking read on a key; if set to 0, there is no timeout
sql.defaults.multiregion_placement_policy.enabled	false	false	default value for enable_multiregion_placement_policy; allows for use of PLACEMENT RESTRICTED
sql.defaults.on_update_rehome_row.enabled	true	true	default value for on_update_rehome_row; enables ON UPDATE rehome_row() expressions to trigger on updates
sql.defaults.optimizer_use_histograms.enabled	true	true	default value for optimizer_use_histograms session setting; enables usage of histograms in the optimizer by default
sql.defaults.optimizer_use_multicol_stats.enabled	true	true	default value for optimizer_use_multicol_stats session setting; enables usage of multi-column stats in the optimizer by default
sql.defaults.override_alter_primary_region_in_super_region.enabled	false	false	default value for override_alter_primary_region_in_super_region; allows for altering the primary region even if the primary region is a member of a super region
sql.defaults.override_multi_region_zone_config.enabled	false	false	default value for override_multi_region_zone_config; allows for overriding the zone configs of a multi-region table or database
sql.defaults.prefer_lookup_joins_for_fks.enabled	false	false	default value for prefer_lookup_joins_for_fks session setting; causes foreign key operations to use lookup joins when possible
sql.defaults.primary_region			if not empty, all databases created without a PRIMARY REGION will implicitly have the given PRIMARY REGION
sql.defaults.propagate_input_ordering.enabled	false	false	default value for the experimental propagate_input_ordering session variable
sql.defaults.reorder_joins_limit	8	8	default number of joins to reorder
sql.defaults.require_explicit_primary_keys.enabled	false	false	default value for requiring explicit primary keys in CREATE TABLE statements
sql.defaults.results_buffer.size	16 KiB	16 KiB	default size of the buffer that accumulates results for a statement or a batch of statements before they are sent to the client. This can be overridden on an individual connection with the 'results_buffer_size' parameter. Note that auto-retries generally only happen while no results have been delivered to the client, so reducing this size can increase the number of retriable errors a client receives. On the other hand, increasing the buffer size can increase the delay until the client receives the first result row. Updating the setting only affects new connections. Setting to 0 disables any buffering.
sql.defaults.serial_normalization	rowid	rowid	default handling of SERIAL in table definitions [rowid = 0, virtual_sequence = 1, sql_sequence = 2, sql_sequence_cached = 3, unordered_rowid = 4, sql_sequence_cached_node = 5]
sql.defaults.serial_sequences_cache_size	256	256	the default cache size when the session's serial normalization mode is set to cached sequencesA cache size of 1 means no caching. Any cache size less than 1 is invalid.
sql.defaults.statement_timeout	0s	0s	default value for the statement_timeout; default value for the statement_timeout session setting; controls the duration a query is permitted to run before it is canceled; if set to 0, there is no timeout
sql.defaults.stub_catalog_tables.enabled	true	true	default value for stub_catalog_tables session setting
sql.defaults.super_regions.enabled	false	false	default value for enable_super_regions; allows for the usage of super regions
sql.defaults.transaction_rows_read_err	0	0	the limit for the number of rows read by a SQL transaction which - once exceeded - will fail the transaction (or will trigger a logging event to SQL_INTERNAL_PERF for internal transactions); use 0 to disable
sql.defaults.transaction_rows_read_log	0	0	the threshold for the number of rows read by a SQL transaction which - once exceeded - will trigger a logging event to SQL_PERF (or SQL_INTERNAL_PERF for internal transactions); use 0 to disable
sql.defaults.transaction_rows_written_err	0	0	the limit for the number of rows written by a SQL transaction which - once exceeded - will fail the transaction (or will trigger a logging event to SQL_INTERNAL_PERF for internal transactions); use 0 to disable
sql.defaults.transaction_rows_written_log	0	0	the threshold for the number of rows written by a SQL transaction which - once exceeded - will trigger a logging event to SQL_PERF (or SQL_INTERNAL_PERF for internal transactions); use 0 to disable
sql.defaults.use_declarative_schema_changer	on	on	default value for use_declarative_schema_changer session setting;disables new schema changer by default [off = 0, on = 1, unsafe = 2, unsafe_always = 3]
sql.defaults.vectorize	on	on	default vectorize mode [on = 0, on = 2, experimental_always = 3, off = 4]
sql.defaults.zigzag_join.enabled	false	false	default value for enable_zigzag_join session setting; disallows use of zig-zag join by default

sql.defaults.optimizer_use_multicol_stats.enabled	true	true	default value for optimizer_use_multicol_stats session setting; enables usage of multi-column stats in the optimizer by default
sql.defaults.override_alter_primary_region_in_super_region.enabled	false	false	default value for override_alter_primary_region_in_super_region; allows for altering the primary region even if the primary region is a member of a super region
sql.defaults.override_multi_region_zone_config.enabled	false	false	default value for override_multi_region_zone_config; allows for overriding the zone configs of a multi-region table or database
sql.defaults.prefer_lookup_joins_for_fks.enabled	false	false	default value for prefer_lookup_joins_for_fks session setting; causes foreign key operations to use lookup joins when possible
sql.defaults.primary_region			if not empty, all databases created without a PRIMARY REGION will implicitly have the given PRIMARY REGION
sql.defaults.propagate_input_ordering.enabled	false	false	default value for the experimental propagate_input_ordering session variable
sql.defaults.reorder_joins_limit	8	8	default number of joins to reorder
sql.defaults.require_explicit_primary_keys.enabled	false	false	default value for requiring explicit primary keys in CREATE TABLE statements
sql.defaults.results_buffer.size	16 KiB	16 KiB	default size of the buffer that accumulates results for a statement or a batch of statements before they are sent to the client. This can be overridden on an individual connection with the 'results_buffer_size' parameter. Note that auto-retries generally only happen while no results have been delivered to the client, so reducing this size can increase the number of retriable errors a client receives. On the other hand, increasing the buffer size can increase the delay until the client receives the first result row. Updating the setting only affects new connections. Setting to 0 disables any buffering.
sql.defaults.serial_normalization	rowid	rowid	default handling of SERIAL in table definitions [rowid = 0, virtual_sequence = 1, sql_sequence = 2, sql_sequence_cached = 3, unordered_rowid = 4, sql_sequence_cached_node = 5]
sql.defaults.serial_sequences_cache_size	256	256	the default cache size when the session's serial normalization mode is set to cached sequencesA cache size of 1 means no caching. Any cache size less than 1 is invalid.
sql.defaults.statement_timeout	0s	0s	default value for the statement_timeout; default value for the statement_timeout session setting; controls the duration a query is permitted to run before it is canceled; if set to 0, there is no timeout
sql.defaults.stub_catalog_tables.enabled	true	true	default value for stub_catalog_tables session setting
sql.defaults.super_regions.enabled	false	false	default value for enable_super_regions; allows for the usage of super regions
sql.defaults.transaction_rows_read_err	0	0	the limit for the number of rows read by a SQL transaction which - once exceeded - will fail the transaction (or will trigger a logging event to SQL_INTERNAL_PERF for internal transactions); use 0 to disable
sql.defaults.transaction_rows_read_log	0	0	the threshold for the number of rows read by a SQL transaction which - once exceeded - will trigger a logging event to SQL_PERF (or SQL_INTERNAL_PERF for internal transactions); use 0 to disable
sql.defaults.transaction_rows_written_err	0	0	the limit for the number of rows written by a SQL transaction which - once exceeded - will fail the transaction (or will trigger a logging event to SQL_INTERNAL_PERF for internal transactions); use 0 to disable
sql.defaults.transaction_rows_written_log	0	0	the threshold for the number of rows written by a SQL transaction which - once exceeded - will trigger a logging event to SQL_PERF (or SQL_INTERNAL_PERF for internal transactions); use 0 to disable
sql.defaults.use_declarative_schema_changer	on	on	default value for use_declarative_schema_changer session setting;disables new schema changer by default [off = 0, on = 1, unsafe = 2, unsafe_always = 3]
sql.defaults.vectorize	on	on	default vectorize mode [on = 0, on = 2, experimental_always = 3, off = 4]
sql.defaults.zigzag_join.enabled	false	false	default value for enable_zigzag_join session setting; disallows use of zig-zag join by default
